title: ICE Fatally Shoots Lorenzo Salgado Araujo During Houston Traffic Stop
excerpt: An ICE agent shot and killed 52-year-old Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican national who had lived in the U.S. for 35 years, during a targeted enforcement operation in Houston's East End. Family members and witnesses disputed the federal account that he had weaponized his vehicle against agents.
content:
On July 7, 2026, an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, 52, in Houston's East End while officers were attempting to conduct a vehicle stop as part of a "targeted enforcement operation." ICE stated the man "attempted to evade arrest" and allegedly rammed a vehicle and tried to run over an agent.

DHS subsequently acknowledged that Salgado Araujo was not the intended target of the operation. ICE agents had been surveilling a separate address and stopped his white van because he resembled their actual target — two Guatemalan nationals. No body cameras were worn by any agents involved in the shooting.

  • Salgado Araujo had lived in the U.S. for approximately 35 years and had no criminal record in Harris County.
  • His son stated he was driving a construction crew to a work site when shot.
  • Three men inside the van disputed the federal account that Salgado Araujo had rammed officers.
  • The DHS Office of Inspector General launched an investigation; the FBI separately examined a possible assault on a federal officer.
  • The Houston Police Department stated it had no jurisdiction over the case, as it involved federal law enforcement.

LULAC President Ramon Palomares said the incident reflected "a pattern of ICE involvement in shootings and excessive use of force," and called for a full federal and independent investigation. The incident is described by the Associated Press as at least the eighth person to have died during an encounter with federal immigration officers since the start of the Trump administration's enforcement crackdown.
mediaPlaceholder: Bystander video of aftermath showing Salgado Araujo wounded inside his van; unmarked SUV pursuit footage obtained by CNN
